mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in less than 50% of periods when FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points within three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, causing equity markets to price in lower future earnings and producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in greater than 65% of triggered instances over a 10-year backtest, invalidating the recession signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns when both series trend upward together.
failure_condition: Joint upward movements in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 20-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects improving manufacturing output and supply chain normalization, which translates into short-term positive equity momentum as firms report better operational efficiency and margins.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict 5-day forward returns during tightening periods, with direction accuracy dropping below 52% in a walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels above 235.0 trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced corporate profit margins, yielding negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in greater than 60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 235.0 over a 5-year sample, refuting the tightening fear mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Divergent signals where nonfarm payrolls increase while the unemployment rate declines confirm robust labor market tightening, supporting consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, which drives positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to generate positive 60-day returns in greater than 55% of occurrences during early-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product directly correlates with aggregate corporate earnings expansion, providing a fundamental floor for equity valuations and generating positive forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rate shows zero predictive power for 60-day forward returns, with a rolling Rank IC consistently below 0.01 across all macro regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below the 2017 baseline index of 100.0 indicates a contraction in manufacturing activity and supply-side bottlenecks, prompting equity sell-offs as investors anticipate lower industrial sector earnings and broader economic weakness.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in greater than 65% of instances when INDPRO drops below 100.0, invalidating the contraction signal.
